name: Spaghetti alla Carbonara
description: A Roman classic of silky egg-and-cheese sauce with crispy guanciale, no cream needed.
instructions: Boil salted water and cook spaghetti until al dente.
Render guanciale in a pan until crispy; remove from heat.
Whisk eggs, yolks, and pecorino into a creamy paste.
Toss hot pasta into the guanciale pan off heat.
Add egg mixture and pasta water to emulsify into a silky sauce.
Season generously with black pepper and serve immediately.
tips: Never add cream — the emulsification of eggs and pasta water is the magic.

name: Margherita Pizza
description: The original Neapolitan pizza with San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, and basil.
instructions: Mix flour, yeast, salt, oil, and water into a dough; knead 10 min.
Let rise 1 hour until doubled.
Stretch dough on a floured surface into a thin round.
Spread crushed tomatoes, tear mozzarella on top.
Bake at maximum oven temp (250°C+) for 10-12 min.
Top with fresh basil and a drizzle of olive oil.
tips: A baking stone or steel dramatically improves the crust.

name: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy Arborio rice with porcini and cremini mushrooms in a parmesan broth.
instructions: Sauté onion and garlic in butter until soft.
Add mushrooms and cook until golden.
Stir in rice and toast 2 minutes.
Add wine and stir until absorbed.
Add broth ladle by ladle, stirring constantly, 20-25 min.
Remove from heat; stir in parmesan and remaining butter.
Rest 2 minutes and serve.
tips: The final 'mantecatura' — vigorous off-heat stirring — creates the signature wave-like texture.

name: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
description: Tender chicken in a velvety tomato-cream sauce, spiced with garam masala and fenugreek.
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, spices, and salt for at least 2 hours.
Grill or broil chicken until charred; set aside.
Cook onion, garlic, ginger until golden.
Add tomatoes and simmer 15 min; blend smooth.
Return to pan, add cream and butter.
Simmer chicken in sauce 10 min.
Finish with dried fenugreek leaves.
tips: Kashmiri chili gives the iconic orange color without too much heat.

name: Aloo Gobi
description: A fragrant dry-style stir-fry of potatoes and cauliflower with turmeric and cumin.
instructions: Heat oil; add cumin seeds until they splutter.
Add onion and cook until golden.
Add garlic, ginger, and tomato; cook until mushy.
Add turmeric, coriander, and salt.
Add potatoes and cauliflower; toss to coat.
Cover and cook on low 20 min, stirring occasionally.
Finish with garam masala and fresh cilantro.
tips: Resist the urge to add water — the vegetables steam in their own moisture.

name: Palak Paneer
description: Creamy spinach curry with cubes of fresh Indian cottage cheese.
instructions: Blanch spinach 2 min; blend to a smooth paste.
Sauté cumin seeds, then onion until golden.
Add garlic, ginger, tomato, and spices; cook 5 min.
Stir in spinach puree; simmer 5 min.
Add paneer cubes and cream; simmer 5 more min.
Adjust seasoning and serve with naan.
tips: Lightly pan-frying the paneer before adding gives it a golden crust.

name: Classic Beef Tacos
description: Juicy seasoned beef in warm corn tortillas topped with pico de gallo and avocado.
instructions: Brown ground beef with garlic, cumin, chili powder, and paprika.
Make pico: dice tomatoes, onion, jalapeño; mix with lime juice and cilantro.
Warm tortillas on a dry skillet.
Mash avocado with lime juice and salt.
Assemble: tortilla, beef, pico, avocado.
Squeeze extra lime on top.
tips: Double-stack tortillas for the authentic street taco experience.

name: Black Bean Soup
description: A smoky, deeply satisfying vegan soup with black beans, cumin, and chipotle.
instructions: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add cumin and paprika; toast 1 min.
Add beans, broth, and chipotle; simmer 20 min.
Partially blend for a creamy-chunky texture.
Simmer another 10 min; season with lime and salt.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with tortillas.
tips: The chipotle in adobo is the secret weapon — adjust for heat level.

name: Tonkotsu Ramen
description: Rich pork-bone broth ramen with chashu pork, soft-boiled egg, and nori.
instructions: Blanch pork bones; scrub clean.
Simmer bones with garlic and ginger 4-6 hours until milky.
Braise pork belly in soy, mirin, and sugar 2 hours.
Soft-boil eggs 6.5 min; marinate in soy-mirin brine.
Cook noodles separately; drain.
Season broth with soy and salt.
Assemble: noodles, broth, chashu slices, egg, corn, nori, green onion.
tips: Vigorous boiling of the bones creates the iconic milky-white broth.

name: Miso Soup with Tofu and Wakame
description: A simple, nourishing Japanese soup with silken tofu, seaweed, and miso.
instructions: Simmer kombu and shiitakes in 1L water 10 min; remove.
Rehydrate wakame in cold water 5 min.
Lower heat; whisk in miso paste (don't boil after).
Add tofu cubes and wakame gently.
Serve topped with sliced green onions.
tips: Never boil miso — it destroys the probiotic cultures and dulls the flavor.

name: Teriyaki Salmon
description: Glazed salmon fillets in a sweet-savory teriyaki sauce, ready in 20 minutes.
instructions: Mix soy, mirin, sake, and sugar into teriyaki sauce.
Pan-sear salmon skin-side up 3-4 min each side.
Pour teriyaki sauce into pan; glaze salmon 2 min.
Baste continuously until sauce is sticky.
Serve with rice and garnish with sesame seeds and green onion.
tips: Pat salmon completely dry before searing for a perfect crust.

name: Classic Hummus
description: Silky smooth chickpea dip with tahini, lemon, and garlic — better than any store-bought.
instructions: Drain chickpeas; reserve liquid (aquafaba).
Blend chickpeas with tahini, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, and salt until smooth.
Drizzle in ice water while blending to achieve desired creaminess.
Taste and adjust lemon and salt.
Serve in a bowl with a well of olive oil and paprika.
tips: Peeling individual chickpeas yields the silkiest hummus imaginable.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a North African breakfast classic.
instructions: Sauté onion and peppers in olive oil until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and chili; cook 2 min.
Pour in tomatoes; simmer 15 min until thickened.
Make wells in the sauce; crack eggs in.
Cover and cook 5-8 min for soft-set eggs.
Garnish with parsley and serve with crusty bread.
tips: A well-seasoned cast iron skillet is ideal for shakshuka.

name: Falafel with Tahini Sauce
description: Crispy fried chickpea fritters served with creamy tahini dipping sauce.
instructions: Pulse soaked (not cooked) chickpeas in a food processor.
Add herbs, onion, garlic, spices; pulse to a coarse paste.
Mix in baking powder and flour; chill 30 min.
Form into balls and deep fry at 180°C for 3-4 min until golden.
Make tahini sauce: blend tahini, lemon juice, garlic, water.
Serve falafel in pita with salad and tahini sauce.
tips: Always use soaked but uncooked dried chickpeas — canned ones will fall apart.

name: Pad Thai
description: Thailand's iconic stir-fried rice noodle dish with tamarind, fish sauce, and peanuts.
instructions: Soak rice noodles in warm water 30 min; drain.
Mix tamarind, fish sauce, and sugar into pad thai sauce.
Stir-fry garlic and shrimp in wok on high heat.
Push to side; scramble eggs.
Add noodles and sauce; toss everything together.
Add bean sprouts and green onion at the end.
Serve with lime wedges, peanuts, and chili flakes.
tips: High heat and a well-seasoned wok are non-negotiable for authentic wok hei.

name: Thai Green Curry
description: Fragrant coconut milk curry with green chili paste, vegetables, and Thai basil.
instructions: Fry curry paste in oil until fragrant, about 2 min.
Add coconut milk; bring to a gentle simmer.
Add kaffir lime leaves, fish sauce, and sugar.
Add tofu, zucchini, pepper, bamboo shoots; cook 10 min.
Taste and adjust sweet-salty balance.
Stir in Thai basil just before serving.
Serve with jasmine rice.
tips: Use full-fat coconut milk — light versions make thin, less flavorful curries.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
description: Sichuan classic of wok-tossed chicken with peanuts, dried chilies, and a sweet-spicy sauce.
instructions: Marinate chicken in soy, wine, and cornstarch 15 min.
Mix sauce: soy, vinegar, sugar, sesame oil.
Stir-fry chilies and Sichuan pepper until fragrant.
Add chicken; cook until golden.
Add garlic, ginger, green onions.
Pour in sauce; toss until coated.
Add peanuts at the end.
tips: The Sichuan peppercorn provides a unique mouth-numbing sensation called 'mala'.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice
description: Classic Chinese-American fried rice with day-old rice, vegetables, and soy sauce.
instructions: Heat wok on high until smoking.
Stir-fry onion and carrots 2 min.
Add garlic, corn, and peas; toss 1 min.
Add rice; break up any clumps and press against wok.
Add soy sauce and white pepper; toss everything.
Drizzle sesame oil; garnish with green onions.
tips: Day-old refrigerated rice has drier grains that fry beautifully without clumping.

name: Coq au Vin
description: Chicken slow-braised in red wine with lardons, mushrooms, and pearl onions — a French bistro masterpiece.
instructions: Marinate chicken in wine with herbs overnight.
Brown chicken in oil; set aside.
Render lardons; sauté mushrooms and pearl onions.
Add garlic and tomato paste; cook 2 min.
Return chicken; add wine marinade and broth.
Simmer covered 60-90 min until chicken is very tender.
Reduce sauce and adjust seasoning.
tips: Use a wine you'd actually drink — the quality goes directly into the sauce.

name: Ratatouille
description: A Provençal vegetable stew celebrating summer produce — tomatoes, zucchini, eggplant, and peppers.
instructions: Salt and drain eggplant 30 min; pat dry.
Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add peppers and cook 10 min.
Add eggplant and zucchini; cook 10 min.
Add tomatoes, thyme, bay leaves; simmer 25 min.
Stir in fresh basil at the end.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
tips: Cooking each vegetable separately before combining builds deeper flavor layers.

name: Classic Beef Burger
description: Juicy smash-style beef patty with all the fixings on a brioche bun.
instructions: Divide beef into 175g balls; do not overwork.
Heat cast iron very hot; add butter.
Smash balls flat with a spatula; season heavily.
Cook 2 min until crust forms; flip.
Add cheese; cook 1 more min.
Toast buns in the beef fat.
Assemble with all toppings.
tips: The smash technique creates a Maillard-crusted exterior that's the hallmark of a great burger.

name: Clam Chowder
description: New England's signature thick and creamy chowder loaded with clams, potatoes, and bacon.
instructions: Render bacon until crispy; reserve fat.
Sauté onion, celery, garlic in bacon fat.
Add flour; stir to make a roux.
Add clam juice, milk, potatoes, herbs; simmer 20 min.
Stir in clams and cream; simmer 10 min (don't boil).
Season and serve with oyster crackers and bacon.
tips: Adding the clams at the end keeps them tender — overcooking makes them rubbery.

name: Greek Spanakopita
description: Flaky phyllo pastry filled with spinach, feta cheese, eggs, and fresh dill.
instructions: Wilt spinach; squeeze out all moisture.
Sauté onion and garlic; combine with spinach, feta, eggs, dill, nutmeg.
Layer phyllo sheets in oiled pan, brushing each with butter.
Add filling; top with remaining phyllo sheets.
Score top; brush with butter.
Bake at 180°C for 45 min until golden.
Cool slightly before serving.
tips: Squeezing every drop of water from the spinach is critical — wet filling = soggy phyllo.

name: Grilled Lemon Herb Chicken (Greek Style)
description: Juicy marinated chicken with oregano, lemon, and garlic — simple and perfect for grilling.
instructions: Blend garlic, lemon juice, oregano, rosemary, oil, salt, and pepper.
Marinate chicken at least 2 hours (overnight preferred).
Bring chicken to room temperature 30 min before grilling.
Grill on medium-high 7-8 min per side.
Rest 5 min before serving.
Finish with fresh lemon squeeze.
tips: Bone-in thighs stay juicy on the grill — breasts overcook easily.

name: Bibimbap
description: Korean rice bowl topped with sautéed vegetables, beef, fried egg, and gochujang.
instructions: Season and cook beef with garlic and soy sauce.
Separately sauté each vegetable with garlic and sesame oil.
Fry eggs sunny-side-up.
Arrange rice in a hot stone bowl or plate.
Arrange all toppings in sections on top of rice.
Place egg on top; serve with gochujang.
Mix everything together at the table.
tips: A dolsot (stone pot) creates crispy golden rice at the bottom — the best part.

name: Lamb Kofta with Tzatziki
description: Spiced ground lamb skewers with a cooling yogurt-cucumber dipping sauce.
instructions: Mix lamb with onion, garlic, herbs, and all spices.
Form onto skewers in cylinder shapes.
Refrigerate 30 min to firm up.
Make tzatziki: mix yogurt, cucumber, garlic, lemon, mint, dill.
Grill kofta 3-4 min per side.
Serve with warm pita, tzatziki, and salad.
tips: Grating and squeezing the onion prevents the kofta from falling off the skewer.

name: Lentil Soup (Shorba)
description: A warming, golden Middle Eastern lentil soup with cumin and lemon — deeply comforting.
instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, and carrot until soft.
Add cumin, turmeric, coriander; toast 1 min.
Add lentils and broth; simmer 25 min.
Blend until smooth.
Finish with lemon juice and olive oil drizzle.
Serve with warm flatbread.
tips: A squeeze of fresh lemon at the end brightens the earthy depth of the lentils dramatically.

name: Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
description: Creamy smashed avocado on sourdough with a perfectly poached egg and chili flakes.
instructions: Toast sourdough until golden.
Smash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Bring water and splash of vinegar to a gentle simmer.
Create a gentle whirlpool; slide egg in.
Poach 3-4 minutes for a runny yolk.
Spread avocado on toast; top with egg, chili flakes, and cilantro.
tips: Use the freshest eggs possible — they hold together better when poaching.

name: Cauliflower Fried Rice
description: A low-carb take on fried rice using riced cauliflower with vegetables and scrambled egg.
instructions: Rice cauliflower in food processor; spread and dry on kitchen towel.
Scramble eggs in oiled wok; set aside.
Stir-fry onion, garlic, carrots 3 min.
Add cauliflower rice; cook on high 5 min.
Add peas, soy, and white pepper.
Return eggs; toss everything.
Finish with sesame oil and green onions.
tips: Drying the cauliflower rice prevents sogginess — moisture is the enemy here.

name: Chana Masala
description: Bold, tangy chickpea curry with amchur (dried mango powder) and a rich tomato-onion base.
instructions: Fry cumin seeds until they pop.
Cook onion until deep golden — don't rush this.
Add garlic, ginger, and spices; cook 3 min.
Add tomatoes; simmer 15 min until oil separates.
Add chickpeas and 200ml water; simmer 15 min.
Stir in amchur powder and garam masala.
Garnish with cilantro; serve with bhatura or rice.
tips: Amchur (dried mango powder) is the ingredient that gives chana masala its signature tartness.

name: Bruschetta al Pomodoro
description: Grilled rustic bread rubbed with garlic and topped with marinated fresh tomatoes and basil.
instructions: Dice tomatoes; toss with olive oil, salt, pepper, and torn basil.
Let marinate at room temperature 30 min.
Grill or toast bread slices until charred.
Immediately rub each slice with raw garlic.
Top with marinated tomatoes.
Drizzle with extra olive oil and optional balsamic.
tips: Only use perfectly ripe, room-temperature tomatoes — refrigerated tomatoes lose all flavor.

name: Lemon Garlic Roasted Vegetables
description: A sheet-pan medley of seasonal vegetables roasted with lemon, garlic, and herbs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C.
Chop all vegetables into similar-sized pieces.
Toss with olive oil, lemon zest, garlic, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on two baking sheets.
Roast 30-35 min until caramelized at the edges.
Squeeze fresh lemon over before serving.
tips: Don't overcrowd the pan — a single layer with space is key to roasting, not steaming.
